question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, objects $A$ and $B$ are both in a state of rest, with masses $m_{A}=5\mathrm{kg}$ and $m_{B}=10\mathrm{kg}$, respectively. $OB$ is horizontal, and $OP$ makes an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the vertical direction. ($g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)
question_structure.sub_question_1: The tension magnitudes of the three thin ropes $O P$, $O A$, and $O B$.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The magnitude of the frictional force between object $B$ and the tabletop.

sub_question_1.step_1: Taking object $A$ as the subject of study, the tension force $F_{T O A}{=}m_{A}g{=}50\mathrm{N}$ can be obtained.
sub_question_1.step_2: Taking the node as the object of study, a force analysis is performed. From the geometric relationship, it is known that: $F_{T O B}{=}F_{T O A}{=}50\mathrm{N}$
sub_question_1.step_3: $F_{T O P}=\sqrt{F_{T O B}^{2}+F_{T O A}^{2}}=\sqrt{2}F_{T O A}=50\sqrt{2}N$

sub_question_2.step_4: Analyze the forces acting on object $B$. The friction force exerted by the table on $B$ is $F_{f}=F_{T O B}=50\mathrm{N}$.

image_captions: The image depicts a physics problem involving three objects: a block labeled B on a horizontal surface, another object labeled A hanging vertically, and a string system. The string connects object A to point O, and another string connects point O to block B and to a wall at point P at a 45-degree angle. The system is at equilibrium.

step_1.physical_theorem: Equilibrium of forces (static equilibrium) and relationship between weight and mass

step_3.physical_theorem: Pythagorean theorem for force vector resolution and composition
